tensorflow/tensorflow.summary: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform that makes it incredibly easy to build, train, and deploy machine learning models at any scale, from research experiments to production applications. With comprehensive tools, stable APIs, GPU support, and a massive community behind it, TensorFlow empowers both researchers and developers to turn ML ideas into reality without reinventing the wheel.

n8n-io/n8n.summary: n8n is a workflow automation platform that empowers technical teams to build powerful integrations and AI-driven automations with 400+ pre-built connectors, code flexibility, and complete data ownership—all while keeping everything self-hostable and under your control. Whether you need visual no-code simplicity or the power to write custom JavaScript and Python, n8n gives you the tools to automate anything without vendor lock-in.

huggingface/transformers.summary: The Transformers library gives you instant access to thousands of pre-trained models for NLP, computer vision, and audio tasks—just a few lines of code to solve problems like text classification, translation, question answering, and more. Whether you're building production systems or experimenting with cutting-edge AI, this is the go-to toolkit that handles the heavy lifting so you can focus on your application.

langflow-ai/langflow.summary: Langflow is a visual platform that makes building and deploying AI workflows effortless—drag-and-drop components to create agents and workflows, then instantly turn them into APIs or MCP servers without leaving the interface. Whether you're prototyping quickly or deploying enterprise applications, Langflow handles the complexity while giving you full access to customize everything with Python.

langgenius/dify.summary: Dify is an open-source LLM app development platform that makes it easy to build, test, and deploy AI-powered applications without needing deep machine learning expertise. Whether you're creating chatbots, RAG systems, or complex AI workflows, Dify provides an intuitive visual builder, built-in tools for data management, and seamless integration with popular LLM providers—all available for free with self-hosting options.

langchain-ai/langchain.summary: LangChain is a flexible framework that lets you build powerful LLM applications by chaining together models, data sources, and tools through a unified interface. It handles the complexity of integrations and abstractions so you can focus on building intelligent agents and AI features without worrying about getting locked into specific technologies as the landscape evolves.

x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-.summary: This repository is a comprehensive collection of over 30,000+ lines documenting the system prompts and internal structures of popular AI tools, giving you a rare peek behind the curtain at how these models actually work. Whether you're researching AI behavior, building better prompts, or just curious about what makes your favorite AI tick, this is the ultimate reference guide for understanding AI from the inside out.

microsoft/generative-ai-for-beginners.summary: Learn generative AI fundamentals through 21 hands-on lessons that take you from zero to building real AI applications, no prior ML experience required. This comprehensive course from Microsoft covers everything from prompt engineering to deploying LLM-powered apps, with code examples and translations available in 50+ languages to help developers worldwide get started fast.

Comfy-Org/ComfyUI.summary: ComfyUI is a powerful visual node-based interface that lets you build and run advanced AI image generation workflows without writing code, supporting everything from Stable Diffusion to the latest models like Flux. Whether you're an artist experimenting with generative AI or a developer building complex pipelines, ComfyUI's modular design and cross-platform support (Windows, macOS, Linux) make it the go-to tool for creative AI workflows.

Shubhamsaboo/awesome-llm-apps.summary: A comprehensive curated collection of practical LLM applications showcasing real-world implementations with RAG, AI agents, multi-agent teams, and voice capabilities across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source models. Whether you're looking for inspiration on what's possible with LLMs or want to learn from well-documented projects, this repo is your go-to resource for discovering and building production-ready AI applications.

pytorch/pytorch.summary: PyTorch is a powerful Python framework that makes it easy to build and train neural networks with GPU acceleration, combining NumPy-like tensor computation with automatic differentiation for deep learning. Whether you're doing scientific computing that needs GPU speed or cutting-edge AI research, PyTorch's intuitive, Python-first design lets you write efficient code that just works.

google-gemini/gemini-cli.summary: Gemini CLI brings Google's powerful Gemini AI directly into your terminal with built-in tools like web search, file operations, and shell commands—perfect for developers who want fast, intelligent assistance without leaving the command line. With a generous free tier (60 requests/min), access to cutting-edge models, and extensibility through the Model Context Protocol, it's the most direct way to leverage AI in your development workflow.

microsoft/markitdown.summary: MarkItDown converts virtually any document format—PDFs, Word docs, images, audio, videos, and more—into clean, LLM-friendly Markdown while preserving structure like headings, tables, and lists. It's the perfect preprocessing tool for feeding documents into AI pipelines and text analysis workflows without the bloat of other conversion tools.

rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch.summary: Learn how large language models actually work by building a GPT-like model from scratch with clear explanations, diagrams, and working code that mirrors the techniques behind ChatGPT. This repo is your hands-on guide to understanding LLMs inside and out, from pretraining to finetuning, with everything you need to start experimenting today.

punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers.summary: This is a curated directory of Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers that unlock AI models' ability to securely interact with databases, APIs, files, and other resources—perfect if you want to extend Claude or other AI assistants with real-world capabilities. Whether you're building AI agents, automating workflows, or connecting language models to your actual tools and data, this comprehensive list helps you discover and implement the right MCP server for your needs.

browser-use/browser-use.summary: Browser Use is an open-source AI agent that automates web tasks by controlling your browser, letting you delegate everything from data scraping to form filling directly to an LLM. Perfect for developers who want to build intelligent automation workflows without wrestling with brittle selectors or complex scripting, all while integrating seamlessly with your favorite AI models like Claude or Gemini.

fighting41love/funNLP.summary: funNLP is a comprehensive Chinese NLP resource library that aggregates nearly all the essential tools, models, and datasets you'll need—from LLMs and ChatGPT frameworks to text processing, knowledge graphs, and domain-specific applications. Whether you're building a production NLP system or learning the ropes, this treasure trove of curated resources saves you countless hours of research and lets you focus on actually solving problems instead of hunting for libraries.

mlabonne/llm-course.summary: This comprehensive course takes you from LLM fundamentals through building and deploying production-ready language model applications, with hands-on notebooks and tools that make advanced techniques like fine-tuning, quantization, and model merging accessible to everyone. Whether you're diving into AI for the first time or scaling models to production, you'll find structured learning paths and practical resources that keep pace with the rapidly evolving LLM landscape.

infiniflow/ragflow.summary: RAGFlow is an open-source Retrieval-Augmented Generation engine that combines RAG with Agent capabilities to create a powerful context layer for LLMs, making it easy to build intelligent document understanding systems at any scale. Whether you're building enterprise search, knowledge bases, or AI-powered applications, RAGFlow simplifies the entire RAG workflow with its intuitive interface and production-ready architecture.

lobehub/lobehub.summary: LobeHub is an open-source platform that lets you create, customize, and collaborate with AI agents tailored to your workflow, supporting everything from multi-model LLMs and voice conversations to a marketplace of pre-built agents you can instantly deploy. Whether you're looking to build intelligent teammates, automate complex tasks, or tap into a growing community of agent builders, LobeHub gives you the tools and flexibility to work entirely on your own terms—no vendor lock-in required.

CompVis/stable-diffusion.summary: Stable Diffusion is a powerful open-source text-to-image model that generates stunning visuals from simple text prompts, running efficiently on consumer GPUs with just 10GB VRAM. It democratizes AI-powered image creation by making cutting-edge generative technology accessible to everyone, from artists and designers to developers building creative applications.

anthropics/claude-code.summary: Claude Code is an AI-powered terminal tool that understands your entire codebase and handles routine coding tasks, code explanations, and git workflows through simple natural language commands. It's the coding assistant you've been waiting for—just describe what you need and watch it execute, saving you hours on repetitive work and context-switching.

vllm-project/vllm.summary: vLLM is a blazingly fast LLM inference engine that dramatically boosts serving throughput through innovations like PagedAttention and continuous batching, making it easy to deploy any popular open-source model with minimal setup. Whether you need state-of-the-art performance, multi-modal support, or distributed inference across diverse hardware, vLLM handles it all while offering an OpenAI-compatible API that integrates seamlessly with your existing workflow.

dair-ai/Prompt-Engineering-Guide.summary: Master the art of getting the best results from language models with this comprehensive guide covering everything from fundamental prompting techniques to advanced strategies like chain-of-thought and tree-of-thoughts reasoning. Whether you're just starting out or building production systems, you'll find practical tutorials, research papers, and proven techniques that help you unlock the full potential of LLMs for any task.

hiyouga/LlamaFactory.summary: LlamaFactory is an all-in-one toolkit that makes fine-tuning 100+ large language models incredibly simple—whether you prefer a zero-code CLI, intuitive web UI, or programmatic control. It's trusted by major companies like Amazon and NVIDIA because it handles the complexity of model adaptation so you can focus on getting your custom AI working exactly how you need it.

labmlai/annotated_deep_learning_paper_im.summary: Learn deep learning by reading beautifully annotated implementations of landmark papers side-by-side with clear explanations — from transformers and diffusion models to GANs, all in simple, well-documented PyTorch code. Whether you're trying to understand how attention works or implement the latest architecture, this actively maintained collection turns dense research papers into digestible, practical learning material.

scikit-learn/scikit-learn.summary: Scikit-learn is the go-to Python library for machine learning, offering a comprehensive suite of tools for classification, regression, clustering, and dimensionality reduction with a consistent, intuitive API. Built on NumPy and SciPy, it's production-ready, extensively tested, and backed by an active community that makes it perfect whether you're just starting with ML or building enterprise-grade systems.

opendatalab/MinerU.summary: MinerU is a powerful open-source tool that intelligently extracts text, tables, and images from PDFs and documents with high fidelity, preserving their original layout and structure. Whether you're working with research papers, reports, or complex documents, MinerU makes it easy to convert messy PDFs into clean, usable data for your projects.

Mintplex-Labs/anything-llm.summary: AnythingLLM is a full-stack app that lets you chat with your documents and data by turning any content into context that LLMs can reference, with support for multiple AI models, vector databases, and multi-user management. It's the all-in-one solution for building intelligent document-based AI applications without the typical setup headaches, available as both a self-hosted and desktop application.

ultralytics/ultralytics.summary: Ultralytics YOLO is the industry-leading computer vision framework that makes state-of-the-art object detection, segmentation, tracking, and pose estimation accessible to everyone with a simple, unified API. Whether you're building real-time applications or experimenting with cutting-edge models, YOLO delivers blazing-fast performance and remarkable accuracy without the complexity—just a few lines of code to get started!

FlowiseAI/Flowise.summary: Flowise is a no-code platform that lets you visually build and deploy AI agents by dragging and dropping components, making it easy to create complex LLM workflows without writing code. With support for multiple integrations, self-hosting options, and a simple one-command setup, you can get your AI agents running in minutes whether you're a developer or non-technical builder.

upstash/context7.summary: Context7 MCP keeps your AI coding assistant in sync with the latest documentation and code examples by pulling real-time, version-specific info directly into your prompts — no more hallucinated APIs or outdated solutions. Just add "use context7" to any prompt and get accurate, current guidance for the libraries and frameworks you're actually using.

run-llama/llama_index.summary: LlamaIndex is a data framework that connects your large language models to external data sources, enabling you to build smarter AI applications that can reason over your documents, databases, and APIs. Whether you're augmenting ChatGPT with your own knowledge or building production-ready RAG systems, LlamaIndex provides the tools to ingest, index, and retrieve data efficiently at scale.

ggml-org/whisper.cpp.summary: Whisper.cpp brings OpenAI's powerful speech-to-text model to life with blazingly fast C/C++ inference that runs efficiently on everything from iPhones to Raspberry Pis—no dependencies required. It's the perfect foundation for building offline, on-device voice applications with support for multiple hardware accelerators and platforms.

jeecgboot/JeecgBoot.summary: JeecgBoot is an enterprise-grade AI-powered low-code platform that combines intelligent agents with visual development tools to help teams build business applications 70% faster without writing repetitive code. It includes built-in support for AI chatbots, workflow automation, reporting, dashboards, and integrates with popular AI models like ChatGPT and DeepSeek, making it perfect for rapid prototyping of enterprise systems like ERP, CRM, and OA platforms.

apache/airflow.summary: Apache Airflow is a powerful open-source platform for orchestrating complex workflows and data pipelines as code, letting you define, schedule, and monitor tasks with incredible flexibility and control. Whether you're managing ETL jobs, machine learning workflows, or intricate data dependencies, Airflow's intuitive DAG-based approach makes it easy to build reliable, scalable automation that works at enterprise scale.

streamlit/streamlit.summary: Turn your Python scripts into beautiful, interactive web apps in minutes without touching HTML, CSS, or JavaScript—Streamlit handles all the web plumbing so you can focus on data and logic. With live editing, instant feedback, and a thriving community, it's the fastest way to prototype dashboards, reports, and data apps that actually impress people.

mudler/LocalAI.summary: LocalAI is a free, open-source OpenAI alternative that lets you run large language models and generate images locally on consumer-grade hardware without needing a GPU, while offering a drop-in compatible REST API. Perfect for developers who want full privacy, zero API costs, and complete control over their AI infrastructure.

milvus-io/milvus.summary: Milvus is a high-performance vector database that efficiently organizes and searches massive amounts of unstructured data like text and images, powering modern AI applications at scale. Built for Kubernetes and written in Go/C++, it handles billions of vectors with hardware-accelerated search, real-time updates, and horizontal scalability—plus there's a lightweight Milvus Lite version you can install with pip for instant local prototyping.

ray-project/ray.summary: Ray is a unified framework that lets you scale Python and ML applications from your laptop to a cluster with minimal code changes, handling everything from distributed training and hyperparameter tuning to reinforcement learning and model serving. Whether you're working with massive datasets or compute-intensive AI workloads, Ray eliminates infrastructure complexity and lets you focus on building amazing applications.

CherryHQ/cherry-studio.summary: Cherry Studio is a desktop AI client that lets you seamlessly chat with multiple AI models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, and more) all in one beautiful, unified interface. Whether you're building with AI or just exploring, it's your all-in-one workspace to experiment, compare responses, and get the most out of different models without switching between apps.

lm-sys/FastChat.summary: FastChat is a complete open platform that lets you train, serve, and evaluate large language models with battle-tested infrastructure that powers Chatbot Arena's 10+ million requests and 1.5M human preference votes. It gives you everything from distributed multi-model serving with OpenAI-compatible APIs to state-of-the-art models like Vicuna, plus the tools and datasets to benchmark your own LLMs against the community.

ToolJet/ToolJet.summary: ToolJet is an open-source low-code platform that lets you build internal tools, workflows, and AI agents visually without writing much code—with 80+ integrations to databases, APIs, and SaaS tools already built in. Whether you're self-hosting or using the cloud version, you get a powerful drag-and-drop builder, real-time collaboration, and the flexibility to add custom logic with JavaScript or Python whenever you need it.

BerriAI/litellm.summary: LiteLLM is a unified SDK and AI Gateway that lets you call 100+ language models with a single OpenAI-compatible interface, eliminating vendor lock-in and simplifying multi-LLM workflows. Whether you're building Python applications or running a proxy server, it gives you the flexibility to swap between providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, and Bedrock without rewriting your code.

huggingface/pytorch-image-models.summary: PyTorch Image Models (timm) is a comprehensive library that gives you access to hundreds of state-of-the-art pre-trained computer vision models, complete with training scripts, inference utilities, and the latest research implementations all in one place. Whether you're building a production image classifier or experimenting with cutting-edge architectures, timm eliminates the hassle of hunting down models across papers and repos while keeping you on the bleeding edge of vision research.

lllyasviel/ControlNet.summary: ControlNet lets you precisely guide image generation in Stable Diffusion by adding spatial conditions like edges, poses, or depth maps—giving you fine-grained control over diffusion models without destroying their quality or requiring massive computing resources. It's a game-changer for developers who want production-ready conditional image generation that's trainable on modest hardware and compatible with community models.

huggingface/diffusers.summary: Diffusers is the go-to library for working with state-of-the-art diffusion models—generate stunning images, audio, and 3D structures with just a few lines of code, whether you're running inference or training custom models. It's built on a philosophy of usability and modularity, giving you easy access to pretrained pipelines while staying flexible enough to customize and experiment with schedulers and building blocks to suit your creative vision.

open-mmlab/mmdetection.summary: MMDetection is a comprehensive object detection framework that makes it easy to build, train, and deploy state-of-the-art detection models with pre-built components and a massive model zoo. Whether you're tackling YOLO, Faster R-CNN, or cutting-edge architectures, this toolbox handles the heavy lifting so you can focus on your vision problems instead of reinventing the wheel.

block/goose.summary: Goose is a local, open-source AI agent that autonomously handles complex engineering tasks—from building projects from scratch to debugging and orchestrating workflows—all while working with any LLM of your choice. If you're tired of context-switching and want an intelligent assistant that actually executes end-to-end development work on your machine, goose lets you focus on innovation while it handles the heavy lifting.

microsoft/graphrag.summary: GraphRAG is a powerful data pipeline that transforms unstructured text into structured knowledge graphs using LLMs, unlocking deeper reasoning and discovery capabilities on your private data. If you're frustrated with generic RAG systems that struggle with complex queries, GraphRAG's graph-based memory structures offer a smarter way to help LLMs truly understand and reason about your documents.

PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR.summary: PaddleOCR is a powerful, easy-to-use open-source library that extracts text from images with impressive accuracy across 80+ languages and multiple document types. Whether you're building document scanning apps, automating data entry, or processing receipts and forms, this battle-tested tool (trusted by 6,000+ projects) gives you production-ready OCR in just a few lines of Python code.

zhayujie/chatgpt-on-wechat.summary: Turn your WeChat into a supercharged AI assistant with this comprehensive agent framework that brings ChatGPT directly to your messaging apps, complete with task planning, long-term memory, and multi-model support. Whether you're running it locally or on a server, it seamlessly integrates with WeChat, Feishu, DingTalk, and more while supporting OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, and dozens of other leading AI models.

photoprism/photoprism.summary: PhotoPrism is a privacy-first, AI-powered photo management app that you can self-host at home or on your own server, giving you complete control over your picture library without relying on cloud giants. It automatically organizes, tags, and searches your photos with smart features like facial recognition, location mapping, and powerful filters—all while keeping your memories completely private and secure.

roboflow/supervision.summary: Supervision is a Python library that provides battle-tested, reusable computer vision tools for working with detection, segmentation, and classification models from any framework. It handles the tedious stuff—loading datasets, visualizing detections on images and videos, filtering objects in zones—so you can focus on building awesome vision applications faster.

servo/servo.summary: Servo is a blazingly fast, parallel web browser engine written in Rust that's pushing the boundaries of what's possible in browser technology through fearless concurrency and memory safety. If you're passionate about web standards, performance, or want to contribute to a cutting-edge open-source project that's reshaping how browsers work, Servo is your chance to make a real impact on the future of the web.

asgeirtj/system_prompts_leaks.summary: This repository aggregates leaked and publicly available system prompts from major AI models like Claude, providing developers with direct access to the underlying instructions that shape AI behavior. It's a valuable resource for understanding how AI systems are prompted, reverse-engineering behavior patterns, and improving your own prompt engineering skills.

anthropics/prompt-eng-interactive-tutori.summary: Learn prompt engineering through hands-on practice with Anthropic's interactive tutorial, covering everything from basic structures to advanced techniques that help you master Claude and avoid common pitfalls. Work through nine structured chapters with built-in playgrounds to experiment in real-time, so you'll actually understand *why* certain prompts work better than others.

netdata/netdata.summary: Netdata gives you real-time, granular visibility into every metric across your entire infrastructure with a beautiful interactive dashboard that updates every second. It's lightweight, open-source, and so addictive that once you start using it, you'll never want to monitor systems any other way again.

janhq/jan.summary: Jan is a desktop app that lets you run powerful open-source AI models locally on your machine with complete privacy and control—no subscriptions, no data sent to the cloud. Whether you want to use local models like Llama and Gemma or connect to cloud providers, Jan gives you a sleek, easy-to-use interface to chat, create custom assistants, and even run an OpenAI-compatible API server.

HKUDS/LightRAG.summary: LightRAG is a lightweight, production-ready framework that makes Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) fast and simple, letting you build intelligent systems that pull from your own data without the complexity of traditional approaches. With built-in support for knowledge graphs, multiple LLMs, and evaluation tools like RAGAS and Langfuse, it's perfect for developers who want RAG to just work without the overhead.

bytedance/UI-TARS-desktop.summary: UI-TARS Desktop is a powerful multimodal AI agent application that lets you automate desktop and browser tasks by seeing and interacting with your screen just like a human would. With support for local and remote operators powered by the cutting-edge UI-TARS model, you can finally hand off tedious UI workflows to an intelligent agent that actually understands what it's clicking.

microsoft/playwright-mcp.summary: An MCP server that supercharges LLMs with browser automation by using Playwright's accessibility tree instead of screenshots—letting AI agents interact with web pages intelligently and deterministically without needing vision models. Perfect for building autonomous workflows, self-healing tests, and exploratory automation that require persistent browser context and deep page understanding.

Budibase/budibase.summary: Budibase is an open-source low-code platform that lets you build beautiful, performant business apps (forms, portals, approval workflows) in a fraction of the time, with support for multiple data sources and extensibility for developers. Skip the tedious UI boilerplate and focus on what matters—Budibase handles the heavy lifting with pre-built components, automation, and integrations so you can ship real applications without the headache.

github/github-mcp-server.summary: GitHub MCP Server empowers AI assistants and agents to deeply interact with GitHub—reading code, managing issues and PRs, analyzing commits, and automating workflows—all through natural language, turning your AI tools into intelligent GitHub collaborators. Whether you're triaging bugs, reviewing code changes, or orchestrating complex CI/CD workflows, this integration lets you leverage AI to streamline your entire development pipeline without leaving your editor.

firecrawl/firecrawl.summary: Firecrawl turns any website into clean, LLM-ready data with industry-leading reliability, handling JavaScript rendering, proxies, and dynamic content that breaks other scrapers. Whether you need markdown, structured JSON, or screenshots, it powers AI agents and apps with real-time web context through a simple API.

gradio-app/gradio.summary: Gradio is an open-source Python library that lets you build and share beautiful web interfaces for machine learning models and Python functions in just a few lines of code—no web development skills required. Perfect for quickly turning your AI project into a shareable demo that runs in any browser.

alibaba/nacos.summary: Nacos is a dynamic service discovery and configuration management platform that simplifies building cloud-native applications and microservices by centralizing service registration, health checking, and configuration management across your entire infrastructure. Whether you're running Dubbo, gRPC, Spring Cloud, or Kubernetes services, Nacos eliminates deployment friction by letting you update configurations and manage services without restarting applications.

KeygraphHQ/shannon.summary: Shannon is an autonomous AI pentester that analyzes your source code and executes real exploits against your web applications and APIs to prove vulnerabilities before they reach production. It closes the security gap between infrequent annual penetration tests by providing on-demand automated security testing that can run against every build or release.

ItzCrazyKns/Perplexica.summary: Perplexica is a privacy-first AI search engine that runs locally on your hardware, combining real-time web results with support for local LLMs and major AI providers while keeping your queries completely private. It delivers cited, accurate answers with smart search modes, multiple source types, and useful widgets—giving you the power of Perplexity without sacrificing your data.

CopilotKit/CopilotKit.summary: CopilotKit is an open-source SDK that lets you build AI agent applications with generative UI, real-time shared state, and human-in-the-loop workflows—perfect for creating next-gen applications where users and AI collaborate seamlessly. With its simple React components and backend tool rendering, you can ship a full-stack agentic app in minutes, whether you're starting fresh or integrating into an existing project.

JuliaLang/julia.summary: Julia is a high-level, high-performance dynamic language purpose-built for technical computing that combines the ease of Python with the speed of C, making it perfect for numerical analysis, data science, and scientific computing. With a growing ecosystem of packages and an active community, Julia lets you write fast, expressive code without sacrificing performance or readability.

oobabooga/text-generation-webui.summary: Text Generation Web UI is a user-friendly Gradio interface that lets you run powerful open-source language models completely locally and privately—with support for multiple backends, image generation, vision models, and web search all in one place. Whether you want to chat with custom characters, generate images, or build an OpenAI-compatible API, this project makes advanced AI accessible without any setup hassle or privacy concerns.

paperless-ngx/paperless-ngx.summary: Paperless-ngx is a powerful document management system that scans and digitizes your physical papers into a fully searchable, organized online archive—letting you finally go paperless. It's open-source, actively maintained by a community team, and includes OCR, tagging, filtering, and a beautiful web interface to help you find any document in seconds.

moeru-ai/airi.summary: Project AIRI is an open-source framework for creating AI-powered virtual characters and waifus with personality, voice, and interactive capabilities inspired by Neuro-sama. If you're interested in building conversational AI companions, virtual streamers, or exploring the intersection of AI and character design, this project gives you the tools and community to bring digital souls to life.

keras-team/keras.summary: Keras 3 is a multi-backend deep learning framework that lets you write code once and run it seamlessly across JAX, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and more—giving you the freedom to pick the fastest backend for your specific model and unlock 20-350% performance gains. Join millions of developers building computer vision, NLP, and other AI solutions faster with Keras' legendary simplicity and power.

microsoft/ai-agents-for-beginners.summary: Learn everything you need to build AI agents from the ground up with this comprehensive Microsoft course designed for beginners, complete with hands-on lessons and multi-language support. Whether you're curious about agent architecture, tool integration, or practical implementation, this resource walks you through real-world examples that'll have you building intelligent agents in no time.

virattt/ai-hedge-fund.summary: This educational project brings legendary investing philosophies to life by orchestrating multiple AI agents—each mimicking icons like Warren Buffett, Charlie Munger, and Cathie Wood—to collaboratively analyze stocks and generate trading insights. It's a fascinating way to explore how different investment strategies can work together through AI, perfect for learning about both machine learning and investment principles without any real money at stake.

lutzroeder/netron.summary: Netron is a powerful, easy-to-use viewer that lets you visualize and understand neural network architectures across virtually every popular ML framework—ONNX, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Core ML, and many more. Whether you're debugging a model, learning how networks are structured, or collaborating with teammates, Netron makes it instantly clear what's happening inside your models with beautiful interactive diagrams.

deepspeedai/DeepSpeed.summary: DeepSpeed is a deep learning optimization library that dramatically accelerates training and inference of large models like LLMs through advanced techniques like ZeRO memory optimization, tensor parallelism, and intelligent offloading. If you're training massive models and hitting memory or speed bottlenecks, DeepSpeed gives you the tools to scale efficiently across GPUs and CPUs while cutting training time and costs significantly.

openclaw/openclaw.summary: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant that runs locally on your devices and integrates seamlessly with all your favorite messaging platforms—WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, and many more—so your AI meets you where you already are. It prioritizes privacy, speed, and that "always-on" feeling of having a genuinely personal assistant that works exactly how you want it to.

mem0ai/mem0.summary: Mem0 is an intelligent memory layer that gives AI assistants persistent, personalized memory to remember user preferences and adapt over time—making chatbots, customer support systems, and AI agents dramatically smarter and more contextual. It delivers 26% better accuracy than competitors while being 91% faster and using 90% fewer tokens, so you get better AI interactions without breaking the bank.

mindsdb/mindsdb.summary: MindsDB is an open-source query engine that lets you build AI agents that answer questions directly from your databases and data sources—no ETL needed—by connecting to 200+ live data integrations and unifying structured and unstructured data. It's perfect if you want to create conversational analytics or semantic search agents without the complexity of traditional data pipelines.

microsoft/qlib.summary: Qlib is a powerful quantitative investment platform by Microsoft that provides end-to-end tools for factor mining, model development, and backtesting in algorithmic trading. Whether you're building predictive models for stock markets or exploring AI-driven investment strategies, Qlib streamlines the entire workflow with production-ready components and now supports LLM-based autonomous agents for automated research and optimization.

666ghj/BettaFish.summary: BettaFish is an AI-powered multi-agent public opinion analysis system that monitors 30+ mainstream social media platforms in real-time to help you break through information bubbles and make data-driven decisions by simply chatting with the system about your analysis needs. With its advanced ensemble of specialized agents, multimodal capabilities, and seamless data integration, it delivers deep insights and predictive intelligence far beyond traditional dashboards—making it an extensible foundation for any data analysis scenario from finance to brand reputation management.

obra/superpowers.summary: Superpowers transforms AI coding agents into autonomous developers by giving them a structured workflow that plans before coding, breaks work into manageable tasks, and orchestrates multi-hour development sprints with minimal human intervention. It's the difference between an AI that jumps straight to writing code and one that thinks through the problem, gets your sign-off on the design, and then executes with the discipline of a junior engineer following a detailed spec.

OpenHands/OpenHands.summary: OpenHands is an open-source AI development agent that automates coding tasks—think of it as having an AI pair programmer that can write code, run tests, and solve problems for you. Whether you want a CLI tool, local GUI, cloud platform, or Python SDK, OpenHands gives you flexible ways to harness AI for software development, from personal projects to enterprise deployments.

code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent.summary: Oh My OpenCode is a multi-model AI agent framework that breaks free from vendor lock-in by orchestrating Claude, GPT, Kimi, and other LLMs together for autonomous coding tasks. Built as a powerful alternative to closed ecosystems, it gives you the flexibility to leverage the best strengths of each model rather than being trapped with a single provider's limitations.

opencv/opencv.summary: OpenCV is the gold-standard, open-source library for computer vision that brings image processing, video analysis, and real-time object detection to your fingertips with industrial-grade reliability. Whether you're building face recognition systems, autonomous vehicles, or creative visual effects, OpenCV's massive ecosystem and 86k+ stars prove it's the go-to tool trusted by developers worldwide.

unslothai/unsloth.summary: Unsloth makes fine-tuning large language models dramatically faster and cheaper — train models 2x faster while using 70% less VRAM, all with beginner-friendly Google Colab notebooks that let you get started instantly for free. Whether you're working with Llama, Qwen, Gemma, or other popular models, Unsloth removes the resource barriers that make LLM customization inaccessible to most developers.

ollama/ollama.summary: Ollama makes it super easy to run powerful open-source AI models locally on your machine with a simple CLI, REST API, and integrations for Python and JavaScript. Whether you want to chat with models, build AI agents, or power your applications without relying on cloud APIs, Ollama gets you up and running in minutes with zero complexity.

affaan-m/everything-claude-code.summary: If you're wrestling with slow AI agents or clunky prompt setups, this production-tested system gives you token optimization, memory persistence, continuous learning, and security scanning to build lean, intelligent AI agents that actually scale. From an Anthropic hackathon winner, it's not just theory—it's battle-tested across multiple AI platforms with 10+ months of real-world optimization baked in.

msitarzewski/agency-agents.summary: The Agency is a production-ready collection of specialized AI agent personalities designed to tackle real-world development tasks—from frontend wizards to DevOps automators—each with deep expertise, proven workflows, and measurable outcomes that actually deliver. Whether you're building React apps, architecting backends, or automating deployments, you can activate the perfect specialist for your workflow and get genuine results instead of generic prompts.

thedotmack/claude-mem.summary: I can't write a summary for this repo because the README appears to be misleading. The GitHub description claims this is a "persistent memory compression system built for Claude Code," but the actual content shows cryptocurrency token links and Solana blockchain references, suggesting this is a crypto project rather than a developer tool. I'd recommend verifying the repo's actual purpose before including it in an awesome-list, as there seems to be a significant disconnect between the stated purpose and the actual content.

microsoft/BitNet.summary: BitNet.cpp is Microsoft's optimized inference framework that makes 1-bit language models blazingly fast and energy-efficient on standard CPUs and GPUs—achieving 2-6x speedups and 55-82% energy savings compared to traditional models. Run a 100B parameter model locally at human reading speed on your own hardware without cloud dependency or expensive GPUs, making powerful AI accessible to everyone.

open-webui/open-webui.summary: Open WebUI is a self-hosted, extensible AI platform that lets you run large language models entirely offline while supporting popular backends like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It's perfect for developers who want full control over their AI deployments without relying on cloud services, complete with built-in RAG capabilities, granular permissions, and a beautiful web interface.

deepfakes/faceswap.summary: FaceSwap is a powerful deep learning tool that makes face recognition and swapping accessible to everyone, whether you're creating visual effects, entertainment content, or exploring AI technology without needing a PhD. With an active community, comprehensive documentation, and both GUI and command-line interfaces, it democratizes advanced AI techniques that were once locked behind academic paywalls and complexity.

danny-avila/LibreChat.summary: LibreChat is an open-source ChatGPT alternative that lets you chat with any AI model—Claude, GPT, local models, and more—all in one unified interface with zero lock-in. Beyond chat, it packs powerful extras like a code interpreter, web search, custom AI agents, and tool integrations, giving you complete control and flexibility over your AI experience.

farion1231/cc-switch.summary: CC Switch is a unified desktop manager that lets you seamlessly switch between multiple AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI all in one place, eliminating the hassle of juggling separate tools and accounts. Built with Tauri for cross-platform reliability, it's the go-to solution for developers who want to harness the power of different AI models without the friction of context-switching between applications.

ComposioHQ/composio.summary: Composio is a universal SDK that supercharges your AI agents with instant access to hundreds of tools and APIs, handling all the integration complexity so you can focus on building intelligent workflows. Whether you're using Python or TypeScript, it seamlessly connects your favorite AI frameworks to real-world actions—from APIs to web services—making it incredibly easy to turn agent ideas into production-ready systems.

labring/FastGPT.summary: FastGPT is an open-source AI Agent platform that lets you build sophisticated LLM applications with visual workflow orchestration, knowledge base management, and built-in tools for data processing—no coding required. Deploy it locally with Docker, use the cloud version, or integrate it into your existing systems via OpenAPI to create production-ready AI solutions for complex use cases.

bytedance/deer-flow.summary: DeerFlow is a powerful open-source agent framework that orchestrates multiple AI sub-agents, memory systems, and sandboxed environments to tackle complex tasks through extensible skills and tools. Whether you're building autonomous research systems or sophisticated multi-agent applications, DeerFlow gives you the infrastructure to create agents that can explore, reason, and act at scale.

microsoft/ML-For-Beginners.summary: A comprehensive 12-week curriculum from Microsoft that teaches machine learning fundamentals through hands-on lessons, quizzes, and real-world projects—perfect for beginners who want to build a solid foundation without getting overwhelmed by theory. With support for 50+ languages and friendly, practical lessons, you'll go from zero to confident ML practitioner at your own pace.

666ghj/MiroFish.summary: MiroFish is a groundbreaking multi-agent AI prediction engine that creates high-fidelity digital worlds from real-world seed data to let you simulate future scenarios and test decisions risk-free. Whether you're predicting policy outcomes, exploring financial trends, or even resolving fictional plot twists, MiroFish transforms "what-if" questions into concrete insights through interactive swarm intelligence.

Lightning-AI/pytorch-lightning.summary: PyTorch Lightning eliminates boilerplate engineering code by automating distributed training, mixed precision, and multi-GPU scaling while letting you focus purely on model logic—think of it as the React for PyTorch that handles all the infrastructure complexity. Whether you're prototyping on CPU or scaling to multi-node clusters, you write the science and Lightning handles the engineering without sacrificing expert-level control.

f/prompts.chat.summary: prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source library of AI prompts, offering thousands of tested examples that work with ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and any modern AI model to help you get better results instantly. Whether you're new to prompt engineering or looking to level up your AI skills, this community-driven resource has you covered with real examples, an interactive learning guide, and tools to find or share prompts that actually work.

ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp.summary: This MCP server bridges AI coding assistants like Claude and Gemini directly to Chrome DevTools, giving your AI agent superpowers for browser automation, debugging, network inspection, and performance analysis. It's the missing link between your AI assistant and real browser control—perfect for building reliable automation workflows, diagnosing tricky frontend bugs, and getting actionable performance insights without leaving your IDE.

qdrant/qdrant.summary: Qdrant is a blazingly-fast vector database engine written in Rust that makes it easy to build AI applications with semantic search, recommendations, and neural-network matching. Whether you're prototyping locally or scaling to production, it handles embeddings efficiently with powerful filtering capabilities and comes with client libraries for every major language.

gsd-build/get-shit-done.summary: GET SHIT DONE is a lightweight meta-prompting system that keeps your AI coding assistant sharp by preventing context degradation as the conversation window fills up—perfect for developers who want Claude Code to actually deliver reliable, scalable results instead of degrading garbage. Trusted by engineers at major tech companies, it strips away enterprise complexity and gives your AI the context engineering it needs to understand your project deeply and execute consistently.

shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code.summary: Learn Claude Code teaches you to build real AI agents by leveraging Claude's capabilities as a trained model—not by chaining prompts or building workflow scaffolding—grounded in the proven history of how neural networks actually learn to perceive, reason, and act. This is your guide to understanding what agents truly are and how to harness them for meaningful engineering work.

explosion/spaCy.summary: spaCy is an industrial-strength NLP library that brings cutting-edge neural networks and pretrained models to Python, making it easy to build production-ready applications for tasks like named entity recognition, text classification, and dependency parsing across 70+ languages. If you need to process text at scale with state-of-the-art accuracy and speed, spaCy is the battle-tested tool that gets the job done right.

TauricResearch/TradingAgents.summary: TradingAgents is a powerful multi-agent LLM framework that brings real-world trading firm dynamics to life, deploying specialized AI agents (fundamental analysts, sentiment experts, technical analysts, traders, and risk managers) that collaborate to make smarter trading decisions. Whether you're researching AI in finance or building automated trading systems, this framework gives you a production-ready foundation to experiment with how different trading perspectives can work together through intelligent agent collaboration.

fishaudio/fish-speech.summary: Fish Speech is a cutting-edge text-to-speech and voice cloning system trained on over 10 million hours of multilingual audio that delivers stunningly expressive, natural-sounding voices across 80+ languages. Whether you're building an app, integrating with an LLM agent, or just experimenting with AI audio, Fish Speech makes it dead simple with command-line tools, a web UI, and Docker support—no complicated setup required.

harry0703/MoneyPrinterTurbo.summary: MoneyPrinterTurbo automates short video creation by generating scripts, sourcing footage, adding subtitles, and composing music—just provide a topic or keyword and get a polished HD video. It supports multiple AI models, customizable subtitles and music, batch generation, and both Web and API interfaces, making viral content creation accessible to everyone.

jingyaogong/minimind.summary: MiniMind is an open-source project that lets you train a tiny 26M parameter language model from scratch in just 2 hours on a single GPU for under $3, complete with full implementations of pretraining, fine-tuning, LoRA, DPO, and reinforcement learning — all written from raw PyTorch with no third-party abstractions. If you've ever wanted to understand how large language models actually work by building one yourself rather than tweaking someone else's, this is your playground.

hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code.summary: Awesome Claude Code is a curated collection of skills, agents, plugins, and tools that supercharge your Claude Code workflow—from prompt injection scanners to session managers that help you work smarter with AI agents. Whether you're building agents, managing permissions safely, or debugging what Claude is doing under the hood, this repo has battle-tested tools to level up your productivity.

ruvnet/RuView.summary: RuView turns WiFi signals into a privacy-preserving perception system that reconstructs human pose, vital signs, and presence through walls using edge AI — no cameras, cloud, or internet required. Built for cheap hardware like ESP32 nodes, it learns locally and continuously adapts, making it perfect for smart homes, accessibility, and security applications that demand privacy and offline reliability.

google-research/google-research.summary: Google Research is a massive collection of open-source code, datasets, and implementations from Google's research teams covering everything from machine learning and computer vision to natural language processing and beyond. Whether you're looking to reproduce cutting-edge research, learn from production-quality implementations, or jumpstart your own projects with battle-tested code, this repo gives you direct access to the innovations powering some of the world's most advanced AI systems.

Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT.summary: AutoGPT is a powerful platform for building and deploying autonomous AI agents that automate complex workflows without requiring deep technical expertise. With both self-hosting and cloud options, plus a visual agent builder interface, you can create intelligent automation solutions in minutes instead of weeks.

hacksider/Deep-Live-Cam.summary: Deep-Live-Cam makes it incredibly easy to create real-time face swaps and deepfakes with just a single image and three clicks—perfect for artists, content creators, and streamers who want to animate characters or add creative flair to live performances. The software runs smoothly on consumer hardware (CPU, GPU, or Mac Silicon) and includes built-in safeguards to ensure responsible use, making it an accessible tool for anyone looking to explore AI-generated media ethically.

continuedev/continue.summary: Continue is an open-source AI agent platform that turns markdown-based checks into enforceable code reviews on every pull request, catching security issues, best practices, and custom requirements before they land in your codebase. It's perfect for teams wanting to scale code quality standards with AI-powered CI/CD integration that's transparent, auditable, and completely customizable.

sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills.summary: Antigravity Awesome Skills is a searchable, installable library of 1,331+ reusable AI coding skills designed for Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and other AI assistants—giving you battle-tested prompts and workflows for planning, coding, debugging, testing, security, and infrastructure work instead of hunting through scattered documentation. Install it in one minute and instantly level up your AI coding assistant with curated skills that actually solve real development problems.

ruvnet/ruflo.summary: RuFlo is an enterprise-grade AI orchestration platform that lets you deploy and coordinate 100+ specialized agents working together seamlessly, powered by Claude and built on battle-tested Rust foundations. If you're building complex multi-agent systems and need fault tolerance, self-learning capabilities, and production-ready infrastructure, RuFlo handles the heavy lifting so you can focus on what makes your application unique.

Snailclimb/JavaGuide.summary: JavaGuide is the ultimate comprehensive resource for Java developers preparing for technical interviews, offering systematically organized knowledge covering fundamentals, collections, concurrency, frameworks, and system design all in one place. With 150k+ stars, it's trusted by thousands of developers to master Java backend concepts through detailed explanations, source code analysis, and interview-focused summaries.

OpenBB-finance/OpenBB.summary: OpenBB is an open-source financial data integration platform that connects multiple data sources and exposes them across Python, Excel, APIs, and AI agents—making it easy to build data pipelines for quants, analysts, and AI applications. Whether you're building research dashboards, training AI copilots, or analyzing markets, OpenBB eliminates the friction of wrangling disparate financial data sources with a unified "connect once, consume everywhere" architecture.

crewAIInc/crewAI.summary: CrewAI is a fast, lightweight Python framework for building autonomous multi-agent systems that work together to solve complex problems, giving you both high-level simplicity and granular control. With CrewAI Flows and an enterprise control plane for monitoring and deployment, you can build production-ready AI automation that scales—trusted by over 100,000 certified developers.

microsoft/VibeVoice.summary: VibeVoice is an open-source voice AI framework from Microsoft that powers both cutting-edge text-to-speech and speech-to-text models, letting you build voice-enabled apps with multilingual support across 50+ languages. Whether you need real-time TTS streaming, long-form audio transcription with speaker identification, or instant integration via Hugging Face Transformers, VibeVoice gives you production-ready models that the community is already building amazing things on top of.

openai/codex.summary: Codex CLI is OpenAI's locally-running coding agent that brings AI-powered development assistance right to your terminal, VS Code, or desktop. Whether you're looking to supercharge your coding workflow with intelligent suggestions and completions, Codex integrates seamlessly with your existing tools and ChatGPT account to help you write better code faster.

langchain-ai/langgraph.summary: LangGraph is a low-level orchestration framework that makes building stateful, long-running agents straightforward with durable execution, human-in-the-loop capabilities, and comprehensive memory management. If you're tired of agents that crash, lose context, or are hard to debug in production, LangGraph gives you the infrastructure to build reliable, observable agent systems that actually work at scale.

QwenLM/Qwen3.summary: Qwen3 is a cutting-edge open-weight large language model family that delivers state-of-the-art performance across reasoning, coding, math, and long-context understanding, with variants ranging from 4B to 235B parameters to fit any use case. Whether you need a compact model for edge deployment or a powerhouse for complex reasoning tasks, Qwen3 offers production-ready solutions with comprehensive tooling for inference, deployment, quantization, and fine-tuning—all backed by excellent documentation and an active community.

badlogic/pi-mono.summary: Pi is a comprehensive monorepo for building AI agents and managing LLM deployments, featuring a unified multi-provider API, an interactive coding agent CLI, and essential tools like agent runtimes and web UI components. Whether you're deploying language models or building sophisticated AI agents, Pi gives you production-ready packages to get started quickly without reinventing the wheel.

ggml-org/llama.cpp.summary: Run powerful large language models efficiently on your own hardware with minimal setup—llama.cpp brings state-of-the-art LLM inference to C/C++ with optimized support for everything from Apple silicon to NVIDIA GPUs, plus flexible quantization options to fit any device. Whether you want a lightning-fast local chatbot, an OpenAI-compatible API server, or cutting-edge performance on consumer hardware, this battle-tested project makes it surprisingly simple and fun to experiment with AI models.

aaif-goose/goose.summary: Goose is a native, open-source AI agent that runs locally on your desktop, CLI, or API—built in Rust and compatible with 15+ LLM providers and 70+ extensions via the Model Context Protocol. Whether you're coding, researching, writing, or automating workflows, goose gives you a powerful, flexible AI assistant that works exactly how you need it to.

NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is a self-improving AI assistant that learns from your interactions, creates and refines its own skills over time, and runs anywhere from a cheap VPS to serverless infrastructure without locking you into a specific model or platform. With native support for Telegram, Discord, Slack, and more, plus a built-in learning loop that builds a persistent understanding of you across sessions, it's like having a genuinely adaptive collaborator that gets smarter the more you use it.

hpcaitech/ColossalAI.summary: Colossal-AI is a powerful framework that democratizes large-scale AI model training by making it dramatically cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced distributed computing techniques. Whether you're training massive language models or fine-tuning existing ones, it handles the complexity of parallelization and optimization so you can focus on your AI innovation.

HKUDS/nanobot.summary: Nanobot is an ultra-lightweight personal AI agent that delivers full agent functionality with 99% fewer lines of code than alternatives, making it perfect for developers who want powerful automation without bloat. It supports multiple LLM providers, messaging platforms, and tools while staying production-ready and easy to customize.

milla-jovovich/mempalace.summary: MemPalace is an open-source AI memory system that stores your entire conversation history verbatim in a searchable, organized structure — achieving a record-breaking 96.6% on LongMemEval benchmarks with zero API calls or subscriptions. Instead of letting AI decide what to remember, you keep everything and navigate it like an ancient memory palace, so nothing gets lost and context never disappears between sessions.

shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice.summary: Master Claude Code with battle-tested patterns for building everything from simple commands to complex agentic workflows—this comprehensive guide covers best practices, real implementations, and orchestration strategies that'll level up your AI development game. Whether you're new to Claude or scaling to production, you'll find hands-on examples, expert tips, and the exact techniques that got this repo trending #1 on GitHub.

MemPalace/mempalace.summary: MemPalace is a free, open-source AI memory system that stores your complete conversation history locally and makes it searchable through a Palace-inspired structure (wings, halls, rooms) rather than letting AI decide what to forget. It achieved a record-breaking 96.6% score on LongMemEval benchmarks by keeping raw verbatim conversations instead of summarizing them, so you never lose context from months of work.

zhayujie/CowAgent.summary: CowAgent is a powerful open-source AI agent framework that combines autonomous task planning, long-term memory, knowledge bases, and a flexible skill system to create a truly intelligent assistant that can think, learn, and grow over time. It supports multiple LLMs, multimodal inputs (text, voice, images, files), and seamless integration with popular platforms like WeChat, Slack, and more—all running 24/7 on your computer or server with the flexibility to customize for your specific needs.

anthropics/claude-cookbooks.summary: A collection of ready-to-use code examples and practical guides that teach you how to build powerful AI applications with Claude's API, from classification and RAG to tool integration and multimodal vision tasks. Jump straight into copy-paste recipes for real-world problems like customer service agents, semantic search, and image analysis—no fluff, just working code you can adapt immediately.

forrestchang/andrej-karpathy-skills.summary: This repo provides a single `CLAUDE.md` file distilling Andrej Karpathy's observations into four coding principles—Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, and Goal-Driven Execution—to dramatically improve how Claude generates code by eliminating assumptions, overengineering, and careless edits. If you're frustrated by AI-generated code that's bloated, breaks things it shouldn't touch, or makes silent wrong guesses, these guidelines transform Claude into a more thoughtful, precise coding partner.

google-ai-edge/mediapipe.summary: MediaPipe is Google's powerful framework that makes it effortless to build and deploy on-device machine learning features for vision, text, and audio across mobile, web, desktop, and edge devices. It comes with pre-trained models, easy-to-use APIs, and tools to customize everything to your needs—so you can add intelligent features to your app without the usual ML complexity.

datawhalechina/hello-agents.summary: Hello-Agents is a comprehensive, hands-on guide that takes you from zero to building real AI-native agents, covering everything from core principles and classic paradigms to implementing your own agent framework and multi-agent applications. Whether you're looking to understand agent systems deeply or become a builder rather than just a user of LLMs, this free Datawhale resource combines theory with practical code to help you master the agent revolution of 2025.

Lordog/dive-into-llms.summary: Dive into LLMs is a hands-on programming tutorial series from Shanghai Jiao Tong University that covers essential LLM skills like fine-tuning, prompt engineering, knowledge editing, and model security—all free and practical with code examples you can run immediately. Whether you're looking to deploy custom models, master advanced prompting techniques, or understand LLM vulnerabilities, this comprehensive guide takes you from beginner to practitioner with clear explanations and working code.

AstrBotDevs/AstrBot.summary: AstrBot is an open-source, all-in-one Agent chatbot platform that seamlessly integrates with your favorite messaging apps to deliver reliable conversational AI. Whether you're building intelligent bots for Discord, Telegram, or other platforms, AstrBot gives you a scalable foundation with an extensive plugin marketplace to customize exactly what you need.

koala73/worldmonitor.summary: World Monitor is an AI-powered situational awareness platform that aggregates global news, geopolitical signals, and infrastructure data into actionable intelligence through an interactive dashboard with dual map engines and real-time risk scoring. Whether you're tracking global markets, monitoring geopolitical developments, or building crisis intelligence tools, this project gives you the infrastructure to process and visualize complex world events at scale.

sansan0/TrendRadar.summary: TrendRadar is a lightweight trend-monitoring assistant that deploys in just 30 seconds and intelligently filters news and information so you only see what actually matters instead of endless noise. With support for multiple notification channels (WeChat, Telegram, Slack, email, and more), AI-powered content analysis, and easy deployment options like Docker and GitHub Actions, it's the perfect tool for staying informed without the information overload.
